extra_model_fields.estimated_effort: small (1-3 hours) for a single engineer familiar with the workbook
priority_tasks: author verification script and update claims_review.md

trace_primary_steps: Planned tabs: Review, Inputs, Calc (trace step 3)
Fallback to manual workbook writer noted in trace (trace step showing module not found)

extra_model_fields.rollback_plan: If a change causes unexpected mismatches, revert to the baseline .xlsx and re-run the verification script to identify the offending change; keep the original file as backup.

Run verification_script.py and confirm derived values match workbook within documented tolerances

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: File opens with openpyxl without errors in a neutral environment.
planned_changes.description: Regenerate and re-save the claims_substantiation_table.xlsx ensuring it is a standard, unencrypted .xlsx compatible with openpyxl/pandas. Avoid macros or nonstandard embedding that might block parsers.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Formula export present and machine-readable (CSV or worksheet).
planned_changes.description: Add a 'Formulas' worksheet that lists every derived cell address and its formula (use Excel FORMULATEXT where available) OR provide a CSV (claims_substantiation_table_formulas.csv) mapping cell -> formula for all derived cells.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Every record row contains provenance text and a confidence value (or a documented reason for missing confidence).
planned_changes.description: Add explicit per-record provenance and per-record confidence columns in the Inputs sheet for all metrics used in calculations. Each provenance entry should cite packet line(s) or source identifiers used to compute the metric.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Script runs in a standard environment and reports 'verification passed' (or documents any mismatches with root-cause notes).
planned_changes.description: Provide a short Python script or Jupyter notebook that reads Inputs, recomputes the derived Calc columns (using the documented formulas), and asserts equality (or small tolerance) vs the workbook's Calc sheet. Include instructions to run and a sample output demonstrating pass/fail.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: claims_review.md includes sheet/cell pointers and verification results; sensitivity section updated if necessary.
planned_changes.description: Update claims_review.md to include explicit sheet-and-cell references for key derived calculations, point to the 'Formulas' export and verification script, and re-run sensitivity analysis if provenance/confidence changes numeric gating.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Screenshots and fallback CSV present as additional verification aids.
planned_changes.description: If environment-specific parser issues persist, include representative screenshots of formulas and a CSV export of Inputs+Calc as fallback for human review.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Resolve verification/reproducibility gap caused by inability to programmatically parse the submitted .xlsx and to confirm per-cell formulas and record-level provenance; preserve existing documented methodology and recommendations while adding artifacts that enable automated verification.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables are high quality: claims_review.md documents governing values, methods, formulas, recommendations, and sensitivity checks. The only material verification gap is inability to programmatically parse/inspect the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable in the grader environment). That prevented automated confirmation that every derived cell contains auditable formulas and that per-record provenance/confidence citations are present inside the workbook. Near-complete credit awarded, but fix required to remove reproducibility/verification friction.
